procedure: Root Canal Therapy and Ceramic Onlay Placement

segments.context: Administer local anesthesia into the mucobuccal fold adjacent to the target molar to ensure patient comfort.

segments.step_name: Decay removal and access cavity preparation
segments.context: Use a high-speed handpiece with a round carbide bur under water spray to carefully remove superficial and deep caries. Ensure you completely eliminate infected dentin while maintaining healthy tooth structure.

segments.step_name: Access opening and pulp chamber exposure
segments.context: Assess the cavity floor with an endodontic explorer to locate canal orifices. Next, refine the access cavity walls and fully expose the pulp chamber floor using a tapered diamond bur to establish straight-line access.
Apply a rubber dam to isolate the treated tooth, preventing moisture contamination and protecting the airway.

Shape and clean the root canals sequentially using endodontic rotary files. Irrigate copiously with sodium hypochlorite solution between file sizes to dissolve necrotic tissue and disinfect the canal space. Finally, dry the canals completely with sterile paper points.
Obturate the clean, dry root canals by fitting gutta-percha master cones coated with sealer. Compact the gutta-percha to ensure a tight, three-dimensional seal of the apical and lateral canal spaces.

segments.step_name: Matrix band application, etching, and bonding
segments.context: Position a Tofflemire matrix band to establish proper contours. Acid-etch the enamel and dentin walls, rinse and dry gently, then apply a dental bonding adhesive, ensuring even coverage before light-curing.

Build up the tooth core by placing composite resin incrementally into the cavity. Carefully adapt each composite layer against the walls to avoid voids, and light-cure each increment thoroughly to establish a solid foundation for the onlay.

Remove the matrix band. Use a tapered diamond bur in a high-speed handpiece to prepare the tooth for an onlay. Reduce the weakened cusps and create a smooth, divergent internal preparation with well-defined margins to ensure a strong path of insertion.
Dispense elastomeric impression material around the preparation to capture a highly accurate master impression of the tooth.
Pre-treat the ceramic onlay's internal surface by applying hydrofluoric acid etching gel, rinsing it, and applying a silane coupling agent to optimize bond strength.
Apply adhesive to the tooth preparation, load the onlay with luting cement, seat it firmly, and clean away any excess cement.
Cure all margins thoroughly with light, then finish and polish the restoration boundaries using polishing cups and points.
